sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem are in decent form, remaining unbeaten in their last 5 matches with 3 wins and 2 draws, scoring consistently. Royale Union Saint-Gilloise, however, have been dominant, winning 3 of their last 5 matches, including strong victories, though they suffered a heavy 0-4 loss to Inter in Europe. Their league form remains impressive as they lead the standings with 29 points.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Royale Union Saint-Gilloise have a perfect record against SV Zulte Waregem in recent encounters, winning all 5 of the last head-to-head matches, including a 4-0 thrashing in their most recent meeting. Zulte Waregem have struggled to score against Union, failing to keep a clean sheet in these games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Royale Union Saint-Gilloise top the league with 24 goals scored and just 6 conceded, showcasing a balanced attack and the best defense. SV Zulte Waregem, sitting 6th with 18 points, have scored 18 goals but conceded 15, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. Union’s superior possession and chance creation stats give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Zulte Waregem, Jeppe Erenbjerg has been in fine form, netting 3 goals in the last 5 matches with a 7.52 Sofascore rating. Union’s Kevin Rodriguez matches Erenbjerg’s 6 goals this season and remains a constant threat upfront despite a missing key forward, Raul Florucz.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Zulte Waregem’s strength lies in aerial duels (54.9% won, 2nd in league) and fast breaks (1.75 per game), which could exploit Union’s backline if caught off-guard. Union, however, excel in final-third efficiency and often score late (50% of goals after the 76th minute), potentially capitalizing on any fatigue from the hosts.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Royale Union Saint-Gilloise’s head-to-head dominance and superior league position, along with Sofascore’s 78% probability for an away win, they are clear favorites. Zulte Waregem’s recent form offers hope for a competitive game, but Union’s attacking depth and defensive solidity are likely to prevail.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Royale Union Saint-Gilloise leads SV Zulte Waregem by a score of 1-0 in this matchup at Elindus Arena. The away side has shown dominance in several key areas, including ball possession and creating attacking opportunities. Despite Zulte Waregem's efforts to push forward, they have struggled to convert their chances into goals. Union Saint-Gilloise capitalized on their attacking intent with two well-taken goals late in the half, showcasing their clinical finishing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Royale Union Saint-Gilloise has controlled much of the game, dictating play with higher possession and significantly more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to their opponents. Their ability to create big chances has been evident, even if they've missed some opportunities. On the other hand, SV Zulte Waregem has shown resilience in duels, winning a strong percentage of both aerial and ground challenges. However, their lack of creativity in the final third has left them without a goal, despite some promising moments in attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players so far
halftimeSections.text: For Union Saint-Gilloise, Kevin Rodriguez has been a standout performer, finding the back of the net and posing a constant threat upfront. Marc Giger also made his mark with a well-timed goal just before the break, showing composure in front of goal. On Zulte Waregem's side, Stavros Gavriel has been a bright spot, getting into dangerous positions and contributing with a shot on target. Players like Anosike Ementa and Jeppe Erenbjerg have shown potential to create openings but need to be more clinical in the final moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Union Saint-Gilloise showing early intent to take control, pushing forward with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes. Zulte Waregem responded with a sustained period of pressure, enjoying a significant shift in momentum around the early to mid stages of the half. They pressed hard to build play in the opposition half but couldn’t maintain the intensity. Union Saint-Gilloise regained dominance as the half progressed, with prolonged attacking phases leading to their goals, especially in the latter stages where their momentum peaked.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Defensive concerns
halftimeSections.text: Zulte Waregem's defense has held firm in certain aspects, particularly in aerial duels, but they've been exposed during Union's quick transitions, leading to the two goals conceded. The home side needs to tighten up at the back to prevent further damage. Union Saint-Gilloise, while more solid overall, has shown vulnerability in physical challenges, losing a notable portion of duels. Both teams have disciplinary issues to address, with yellow cards already impacting their approach to the game.
